steps.title: Find the GCF of all three terms
steps.content:
The polynomial is $4x^2 + 52x + 28$. Check divisibility of each coefficient by small integers:
$4 = 4 \cdot 1$, $52 = 4 \cdot 13$, $28 = 4 \cdot 7$.
The greatest common factor of 4, 52, and 28 is $4$.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Write the polynomial with 4 factored out:
$$4x^2 + 52x + 28 = 4(x^2 + 13x + 7)$$
So $4$ is a factor of the polynomial.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $4x^2$ — this is not a factor of the polynomial; dividing $4x^2 + 52x + 28$ by $4x^2$ does not yield a polynomial. Eliminated.
  • B: $7x$ — $7x$ does not divide $4x^2$ evenly (4 is not divisible by 7). Eliminated.
  • C: $4$ — divides every term: $4x^2 \div 4 = x^2$, $52x \div 4 = 13x$, $28 \div 4 = 7$. Valid factor.
  • D: $52x$ — $52x$ does not divide $4x^2$ evenly. Eliminated.
That matches Choice C — 4.
